untranslatable_reason: This signal is a FILTER/AVOIDANCE rule rather than an entry rule. It specifies conditions to AVOID trading (do not enter immediately after climax), not conditions to enter. The core requirement—detecting a 'climactic price move' as a spike in range/volatility relative to recent history—requires comparison of current bar range against a rolling historical distribution, which cannot be expressed using only the allowed point-in-time indicators (atr_7, atr_14, atr_20, true_range, hvol_20, bb_bandwidth). Climax detection requires either: (1) a percentile-ranked volatility indicator, (2) standard deviation of range over N bars, or (3) dynamic threshold calculation. Additionally, 'trend_maturity_minimum_bars' requires counting consecutive bars in the same direction—a stateful property not available in the indicator set. The 'bars_to_wait_after_climax' parameter requires a time-gated entry lock, which is a backtest engine feature, not a condition rule.
translation_notes: Signal GRIMES_DRY_3_0 is best implemented as a (1) pre-filter that flags climax bars using custom volatility spike detection, then (2) a hold/wait timer on entry orders for N bars post-climax. This cannot be captured as entry/exit conditions alone. Recommend: implement climax detection in preprocessing layer, tag bars with climax_detected flag, then add entry_lock_bars=N parameter to engine.
